question.label: Escalate vs batch
question.name: What would a useful escalate-versus-batch guide need to cover?
question.description: Turns the missing escalation guide into concrete onboarding support the manager can provide.

answer: The runbook helped, but some practical handoff details only show up during live incidents.

assessment.note: He names a specific onboarding gap: practical incident handoff details are missing from docs and only learned live, which is strong clarity risk and mild wellbeing strain; [THREAD-DEFERRED] practical handoff details during live incidents.

new_queue.label: Team contact
new_queue.name: Who have you had the most useful direct contact with so far, Riley?

new_queue.label: Support needed
new_queue.name: What would be most useful from me over the next couple of weeks, Riley?

new_queue.label: Next-step landing
new_queue.name: Given what we've covered, what should we tighten up before our next check-in?

issues: coverage: injected wellbeing into next question (0 touches after turn 6)

history.answer_excerpt: The runbook helped, but some practical handoff details only show up during live incidents.
I am comfortable reading the services, but I am less sure how decisions are really made here.
I can trace most request paths now, though I still miss historical context behind certain tradeoffs.
I have not been fully clear on who is on point for each domain when priorities conflict.
I am trying not to interrupt people, so sometimes I keep questions in a notes doc instead of asking.
I would benefit from a clearer guide for what to escalate quickly versus what to batch.
The runbook helped, but some practical handoff details only show up during live incidents.
